title: References and Echoes in 30 to Other Things
display_title: References and Echoes in 30 to Other Things
wikitext:
[]: [SPOILERS] References and Echoes in 3.0 to Other Things             Quote Post by michaelovan » Sat Nov 17, 2012 10:35 pm My favorite scenes in 3.0, which to me recalled Shinji and Asuka's video game sync training, were SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Seele00TextOnly » Sat Nov 17, 2012 11:12 pm ...

[]:             Quote Post by michaelovan » Sun Nov 18, 2012 1:14 am Seele00TextOnly wrote: I like Asuka dragging Shinji paralleling Misato dragging him in EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 18, 2012 4:14 am Heh, what about parallels to other works the creative team has endeavored upon? Expand this thread's scope, or make another?

[]:             Quote Post by EvangelionFan » Sun Nov 18, 2012 4:17 am It is early enough that we can include the parallels and references to other works in this thread without it becoming overpowering or whatnot.

[]:             Quote Post by Xard » Mon Nov 19, 2012 4:01 pm according to J-List guy there's quite few shout outs to Anno's other works : The film is loaded with visual and story elements Hideaki Anno has used in his past Gainax works, and I had a lot of fun picking them out as they danced by on the screen. In fact, while you're waiting for the film to get its inevitable international release, you might hunt down Anno's Gunbuster: Aim for the Top and Nadia of the Mysterious Seas and watch (or re-watch) them, to better enjoy the visual presence of the third Eva film when you see it.

[]:             Quote Post by dummysystem » Mon Nov 19, 2012 11:49 pm Xard wrote: according to J-List guy there's quite few shout outs to Anno's other works : I could swear there was topic for "references to other anime" but can't find it anymore

[]:             Quote Post by Ornette » Tue Nov 20, 2012 12:18 am dummysystem wrote: I mentioned in another thread that there is a Nadia's song in the trailer and I'm pretty sure this pose refers to some other anime but can't find which one http://i45.tinypic.com/awpdeo.jpg

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Tue Nov 20, 2012 12:53 am Using Mark.06 as a trap for the 12th Angel seems to mirror the Adam/Lilith switcheroo pulled on Kaworu in the original series. The other shoutouts/references I've noticed are all Nadia related. The borrowed music. Then there's the Wunder itself; those engine/gun banks on either side of the body bear striking resemblance to the Nautilus with a dish strapped on top. Misato herself is seeming like a big shoutout to Captain Nemo, both in uniform and personality. She even borrows his hat-obscures-one-eye pose.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Nov 20, 2012 1:33 am BlueBasilisk wrote: Using Mark.06 as a trap for the 12th Angel seems to mirror the Adam/Lilith switcheroo pulled on Kaworu in the original series.

[]:             Quote Post by BlueBasilisk » Tue Nov 20, 2012 7:36 am I thought one of the synopses said Mk.06 had been used as a trap for the Angel, which was why it was possessed, impaled, and left to rot. I can't find where I read that now, though. Maybe I'm going crazy O.o

[]: Eva Q Soundtrack - Original Track Reworks (Eva and Nadia)             Quote Post by beecass » Sat Nov 24, 2012 10:28 pm A while ago, a member of the forum posted this entertaining video about the backstory and mechanics of Evangelion, and I was watching it today after finding it again - I noticed a piece of music at 10:50 that appears to be the original version of the track that appears in the opening of 3.0 when Asuka is being attacked by the manufactured Angel hiding inside Unit-01's tesseract (at around the 4:30 mark). I can't for the life of me remember whether this song appeared in NGE at all, but this video was made before the release of 3.0 and appears to only use versions of tracks from the NGE soundtrack rather than NME versions. Would anybody be able to tell me what release this particular piece of music is from, and if it corresponds to any particular part of the show? EDIT: After a bit of spelunking, the track appears to be a rework of "Babel no Hikari" from the Nadia and the Secret of Blue Water soundtrack. The use of the track in an Eva-related context before the release of 3.0 appears to just be a strange coincidence. However, as far as I'm aware from what people are saying about the soundrip, there are other tracks from Nadia that are used in 3.0. Does anyone know of any other tracks from Nadia or NGE that have been reworked and used in 3.0?[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sun Nov 25, 2012 12:00 am (Oh, gawd, that video you linked to... Must locate the appropriate thread for gushing about it!) dummysystem wrote: 1 track from Nadia in the trailer, now 2 tracks in this part. At this point, I'm not sure we can say it's a reference (or laziness) of Shiro Sagisu. The music is when the New Nautilus re-appears : [url]http://www.youtube.com/watch?v=VaS6Rt-VF1M[/url]

[]:             Quote Post by beecass » Sun Nov 25, 2012 1:33 am I've been trying to find the scene in Nadia that corresponds with the song to see if there are any callbacks going on, but I haven't been having any luck. In the two Babel-centric episodes in which I thought I would find it, however, the Tower of Babel fires destructive light light in a fashion similar to the artificial Angel, and some of the wider shots appear almost identical to ones in the introductory scene. It may just be Anno's directorial voice shining through in his coverage, but there was enough similarity there to make it feel like a callback.

[]:             Quote Post by dummysystem » Sun Nov 25, 2012 3:13 pm EDIT: Here is a clip of the original scene. Given the context with which the song is used in 3.0, it seems like a pretty deliberate callback.

[]:             Quote Post by beecass » Mon Nov 26, 2012 8:14 am Dummysystem sent me a message concerning Babel no Hikari's context in Nadia and the Secret of Blue Water - and after another look, yep, it looks like it's used in a scene where Gargoyle orders the firing of the Tower of Babel. Give the light play that's consistent between the two sequences, it seems like another callback. I'd encourage anyone who's seen the opening scene of Q to look up Nadia's Babel-centric episodes (7 and 8) to get a look at the commonality between the two (I won't link to a stream here, but Google is your friend). Do we know of any references to at the moment that aren't related to Nadia or Eva itself? Any interesting YAMASHITA-type moments?

[]:             Quote Post by robersora » Mon Nov 26, 2012 10:09 am In another thread, they said, that Mari was seen eating in her Entry-plug. It reminds me of this: SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Fri Nov 30, 2012 1:31 pm This would be more of a reference to another story, not a callback to NGE itself, but. Another interesting thought from a Japanese Eva fan (Rei RPer, but they have some good thoughts) : King of the Lilin = Gendo Princess = Asuka Shikinami Princess's Attendant = Mari Marvelous Ark = Wunder so would the Prince be Shinji? The concept does remind them of an animated story and a play (maybe a fairy tale?) but they can't remember the title. http://twitter.com/Ayanami_rei_02/status/274577814974504960/

[]:             Quote Post by Kendrix » Sat Dec 01, 2012 7:25 pm As one of the few fans of EoTV, I couldn't have missed these: Shinji: You where the one who showed me that, Nagisa-kun! A world where nothing can be changed! Or here, word-for-word: Shinji: So this is my father's work? One of the most obvious ones is, of course, the episode 23-ish parallelism of Shinji's 1st meeting with the next Rei with one of the first few meetings with the second one. Even if different scenes were used, not the "hospital corridor". He wakes up in hospital, and she's beside him - this time without a book. Or the naked scene, reprise. I also liked how that one line was reversed. Ep 23!Rei: "...Did I save you?" Q!Shinji: "...Did I save you?", the latter being almost something like a recurring arc word in Q. The answer is, sadly, no. Also, we finally got our mecha battle! We never really saw Shinji vs. EVA 02 in episode 24, even if he marches there and throws the defeated EVa before Kaworu's feet like a total badass. Cue Q giving us... Shinji versus EVA 02, this time with Asuka in it. But the sad context made me unable to enjoy it.

[]:             Quote Post by Kodaemon » Mon Dec 03, 2012 12:49 pm Didn't know where to put this, I guess this thread is best suited. From obscure 2008 french sci-fi movie, Dante 01: The film begins with an almost abstract shot of a spacecraft's rocket booster: SPOILER: Show
